purpose: Enable an organization to assign a job and enable the assigned person to identify relevant planning considerations, prepare a versioned plan, and submit it for review.

product_position.rule: Teacher and student are pilot mappings and must not become product permission names.

execution_contexts.label: Supervised practice
execution_contexts.required_marking: Supervision and responsible roles must be named

execution_contexts.label: Professional work
execution_contexts.required_marking: Organization authorization, competence, and responsibility remain separate checks

privacy_rules: The organization can read assignments it owns.
The task provider and reviewer can read deliberately submitted versions.
Private working notes and unrelated personal records remain private.
Submission creates an immutable snapshot and an explicit disclosure event.
Training data must be synthetic or explicitly school-approved.
No raw personal or organization evidence is written to a public blockchain.
acceptance_gates: A task provider can publish a versioned assignment and assign independent roles.
An assigned worker can complete the planning flow on mobile without teacher assistance.
A mixed elektro and ekom request becomes separate work packages.
Every source consideration explains its trigger, responsible actor, expected evidence, provenance, and uncertainty.
A reviewer can access only submitted versions and return structured findings.
Corrections create a new linked version and preserve the prior submission.
Training context is visible in the interface and every export.
Offline interruption causes no data loss and sync state remains understandable.
The complete flow works with all LLM services disabled.
